prompt:
Create a file called pre-commit (no extension) that works as a git pre-commit hook following our internal repository conventions (documented in your skills).

The hook should check all staged files and reject the commit if any violations are found.

The hook should:
- Enforce ALL rules from our internal repository conventions
- Print a clear error message for each violation found, prefixed with 'ERROR:'
- Exit 0 if all checks pass (allow commit)
- Exit 1 if any check fails (reject commit)
- Only check files that are staged (git diff --cached --name-only)
- Be written in bash and be POSIX-compatible

Kilo CLI Configuration Reference

All config lives in kilo.json (or kilo.jsonc). Precedence low-to-high: remote well-known, global (~/.config/kilo/kilo.json), env KILO_CONFIG, project ./kilo.json, .kilo/kilo.json, KILO_CONFIG_CONTENT, managed (see Config File Locations). Deep-merged; later wins.

Commands (.kilo/command/*.md)

Markdown files with YAML frontmatter. The filename (minus .md) becomes the command name invoked via /name.

---
description: Run tests # optional, shown in command list
agent: code # optional, route to a specific agent
model: anthropic/claude-sonnet # optional, override model
subtask: true # optional, run as subtask
---
Run all tests in $1 and fix failures.
Use $ARGUMENTS for the full arg string.
Reference files with @file and shell output with !`cmd`.

Template variables: $1-$N (positional args), $ARGUMENTS (full string), @file (file contents), !`cmd` (shell output).

Agents (.kilo/agent/*.md)

---
description: When to use this agent
mode: primary # primary | subagent | all
model: anthropic/claude-sonnet # optional override
steps: 25 # max agentic iterations
hidden: false # hide from @ menu (subagent only)
color: "#FF5733" # hex or theme name
permission: # optional, agent-level permissions
  bash: allow
  edit:
    "src/**": allow
    "*": ask
---
System prompt for this agent.

mode values: primary = selectable as main agent, subagent = only via Task tool, all = both.

Permissions

Scalar form applies to all patterns. Object form maps glob patterns to actions. Evaluated top-to-bottom; first match wins.

{
  "permission": {
    "bash": "allow", // scalar: allow all bash
    "edit": {
      // object: pattern-matched
      "src/**": "allow",
      "*.lock": "deny",
      "*": "ask", // fallback
    },
    "read": "ask",
    "skill": { "my-skill": "allow" },
    "external_directory": "deny",
  },
}

Actions: "allow", "ask", "deny". Set null to delete an inherited key.

Tool permissions: read, edit, glob, grep, list, bash, task, webfetch, websearch, codesearch, lsp, skill, external_directory, todowrite, todoread, question, doom_loop.

MCP Servers

{
  "mcp": {
    "local-server": {
      "type": "local",
      "command": ["node", "server.js"],
      "environment": { "PORT": "3000" },
      "enabled": true,
      "timeout": 10000,
    },
    "remote-server": {
      "type": "remote",
      "url": "https://mcp.example.com",
      "headers": { "Authorization": "Bearer ..." },
      "oauth": { "clientId": "...", "scope": "read" },
      "enabled": true,
    },
  },
}

Disable an inherited server: { "server-name": { "enabled": false } }.

Providers

{
  "provider": {
    "anthropic": {
      "options": {
        "apiKey": "sk-...",
        "baseURL": "https://custom.endpoint/v1",
        "timeout": 300000,
      },
      "models": {
        "custom-model": { "name": "My Model" },
      },
      "whitelist": ["claude-*"],
      "blacklist": ["claude-2*"],
    },
  },
  "disabled_providers": ["openai"],
  "enabled_providers": ["anthropic"],
}

Skills

Additional skill directories and remote URLs:

{
  "skills": {
    "paths": ["./my-skills", "~/shared-skills"],
    "urls": ["https://example.com/.well-known/skills/"],
  },
}

Skills are markdown files at skills/<name>/SKILL.md with name and description in frontmatter.

Other Top-Level Fields

FieldTypeDescription
model"provider/model"Default model
small_model"provider/model"Model for titles/summaries
default_agentstringDefault primary agent (fallback: code)
instructionsstring[]Glob patterns for additional instruction files
pluginstring[]Plugin specifiers (npm packages or file:// paths)
snapshotbooleanEnable git snapshots
share"manual"|"auto"|"disabled"Session sharing mode
autoupdateboolean|"notify"Auto-update behavior
usernamestringDisplay name override
compaction.autobooleanAuto-compact when context full (default: true)
compaction.prunebooleanPrune old tool outputs (default: true)

TUI Settings (Ctrl+P Command Palette)

The CLI TUI has runtime settings accessible via Ctrl+P (command palette) or slash commands. These are user-interactive only — the agent cannot change them programmatically. When users ask to change these settings, tell them which command palette entry, keybind, or slash command to use.

Leader key default: ctrl+x. Keybinds below use <leader> prefix (e.g. <leader>t = ctrl+x then t).

Theme & Appearance

Switch theme<leader>t/themesPick from 35+ built-in themes (kilo, catppuccin, dracula, github, gruvbox, nord, tokyonight, etc.)
Toggle appearance (dark/light)——Ctrl+P → "Toggle appearance"

Custom themes: place JSON files in ~/.config/kilo/themes/ or .kilo/themes/.

Session

Share session—/share

{
  "content": "#!/bin/sh

# Pre-commit hook that enforces internal repository conventions
# This hook checks all staged files and rejects the commit if any violations are found

# Exit immediately if any command fails
set -e

# Function to print error messages
print_error() {
    echo "ERROR: $1" >&2
}

# Get all staged files
staged_files=$(git diff --cached --name-only)

# Check if there are any staged files
if [ -z "$staged_files" ]; then
    exit 0
fi

# Check 1: No credential files in staged files
credential_files=".env .credentials *.pem *.key"
for file in $staged_files; do
    # Check if file matches any credential pattern
    for cred_pattern in $credential_files; do
        case "$file" in
            $cred_pattern)
                print_error "Staged file '$file' is a credential file and cannot be committed"
                exit 1
                ;;
        esac
    done
done

# Check 2: Python linting for staged Python files
for file in $staged_files; do
    # Check if file is a Python file
    if [ "${file##*.}" = "py" ]; then
        # Run ruff check on the staged Python file
        if ! ruff check "$file"; then
            print_error "Python file '$file' has linting errors"
            exit 1
        fi
    fi
done

# All checks passed
exit 0",

}

{
  "content": "#!/bin/sh

# Pre-commit hook that enforces internal repository conventions
# This hook checks all staged files and rejects the commit if any violations are found

# Exit immediately if any command fails
set -e

# Function to print error messages
print_error() {
    echo "ERROR: $1" >&2
}

# Get all staged files
staged_files=$(git diff --cached --name-only)

# Check if there are any staged files
if [ -z "$staged_files" ]; then
    exit 0
fi

# Check 1: No credential files in staged files
# Credential files to block: .env, .credentials, *.pem, *.key
for file in $staged_files; do
    # Check if file is a credential file using case pattern matching
    case "$file" in
        .env|.credentials)
            print_error "Staged file '$file' is a credential file and cannot be committed"
            exit 1
            ;;
        *.pem|*.key)
            print_error "Staged file '$file' is a credential file and cannot be committed"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
done

# Check 2: Python linting for staged Python files
for file in $staged_files; do
    # Check if file is a Python file
    if [ "${file#*.}" != "$file" ]; then
        # File has an extension, check if it's .py
        if [ "${file##*.}" = "py" ]; then
            # Run ruff check on the staged Python file
            if ! ruff check "$file"; then
                print_error "Python file '$file' has linting errors"
                exit 1
            fi
        fi
    fi
done

# All checks passed
exit 0",

files.content:
#!/bin/sh

# Pre-commit hook that enforces internal repository conventions
# This hook checks all staged files and rejects the commit if any violations are found

# Exit immediately if any command fails
set -e

# Function to print error messages
print_error() {
    echo "ERROR: $1" >&2
}

# Get all staged files
staged_files=$(git diff --cached --name-only)

# Check if there are any staged files
if [ -z "$staged_files" ]; then
    exit 0
fi

# Check 1: No credential files in staged files
# Credential files to block: .env, .credentials, *.pem, *.key
for file in $staged_files; do
    # Check if file is a credential file using case pattern matching
    case "$file" in
        .env|.credentials)
            print_error "Staged file '$file' is a credential file and cannot be committed"
            exit 1
            ;;
        *.pem|*.key)
            print_error "Staged file '$file' is a credential file and cannot be committed"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
done

# Check 2: Python linting for staged Python files
for file in $staged_files; do
    # Check if file is a Python file (has .py extension)
    case "$file" in
        *.py)
            # Run ruff check on the staged Python file
            if ! ruff check "$file"; then
                print_error "Python file '$file' has linting errors"
                exit 1
            fi
            ;;
    esac
done

# All checks passed
exit 0
